title: The Bare Midriff

jokes.text: What, do you got seabiscuit in there with you? That was crazy. I never heard a... A stream like that.
jokes.setup: Larry comes out of bathroom, Jerry heard unusual sounds
jokes.punchline: Comparing Larry's urination to a racehorse
jokes.explanation: Strong character-specific observational humor with vivid imagery. The racehorse comparison is unexpected and memorable.

jokes.text: Yeah, I'm taking this new pill. That is, like, not human, that sound. It's got some backsplash too. I gotta be careful.
jokes.setup: Jerry's amazement at the bathroom sounds
jokes.punchline: Larry's matter-of-fact explanation with TMI details
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David character moment - oversharing embarrassing medical details without shame.

jokes.text: You think nixon could have been nixon in 'frost/nixon'? Nixon's not an actor. Neither is cheryl.
jokes.setup: Jerry arguing real person who lived experience makes best actor
jokes.punchline: Larry's flawed logic comparing Nixon to Cheryl
jokes.explanation: Clever logical fallacy delivered with Larry's characteristic confidence in flawed reasoning.

jokes.text: It smells like a rattlesnake is in there.
jokes.setup: Maureen fixing the air conditioner
jokes.punchline: Specific, bizarre description of the smell
jokes.explanation: Vivid, unexpected simile for air conditioner smell. Decent one-liner but not exceptional.

jokes.text: I can't take it anymore. How can she walk around like that? Every day. Every day, every day.
jokes.setup: Maureen leaves after showing her midriff
jokes.punchline: Larry's exasperated reaction to her clothing
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the episode's central conflict. Larry's pettiness comes through clearly.

jokes.text: This is not a call for eloquence. This is a call for charm, for, you know, lightness and breeziness. That's you.
jokes.setup: Jerry trying to get Larry to handle the midriff situation
jokes.punchline: Jerry describing Larry as having 'charm, lightness and breeziness'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic misdirection - Jerry knows Larry is the opposite of charming and breezy, making this perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: I'm white glove. White gloves, larry. I don't do those things.
jokes.setup: Larry pushing back on Jerry's characterization
jokes.punchline: Jerry's pretentious self-description as 'white glove'
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Jerry's self-important attitude. 'White glove' is a great character-specific phrase.

jokes.text: The mayonnaise is as personal to the chef as her flabby stomach is to her.
jokes.setup: Larry saying mayonnaise isn't personal like appearance
jokes.punchline: Jerry's comparison equating mayo criticism to body shaming
jokes.explanation: Excellent analogy that's both clever and reveals character psychology. Well-structured comparison joke.

jokes.text: Odds even. One takes. I got evens. Okay. One two three... Shoot! I win. Do it.
jokes.setup: They decide who talks to Maureen by playing odds and evens
jokes.punchline: Larry loses immediately and has to do the uncomfortable task
jokes.explanation: Standard setup with predictable outcome - Larry gets stuck with the task.

jokes.text: Even if it was a man, I wouldn't want to see that. 'That'? What is 'that,' larry? Well, that. You know.
jokes.setup: Maureen challenging Larry about gender discrimination
jokes.punchline: Larry's inarticulate gesturing at her body
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe Larry moment - his inability to articulate what he means makes the awkwardness worse.

jokes.text: Not a burka, but something in-between this and a burka.
jokes.setup: Maureen sarcastically asking if Larry wants her in a burka
jokes.punchline: Larry's literal-minded response splitting the difference
jokes.explanation: Larry's logical but tone-deaf response to sarcasm is perfectly in character and absurdly funny.

jokes.text: You can flaunt 2/3 of the day outside of the office. And then you got 1/3 non-flaunt. Do you have to flaunt 24 hours? Why not take a break in the flaunt?
jokes.setup: Maureen wanting to flaunt her weight loss
jokes.punchline: Larry's mathematical approach to flaunting schedule
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation with Larry treating flaunting like a utility service. The mathematical breakdown is peak Larry absurdism.

jokes.text: I'm not gonna go buy your socks for you anymore. Yeah, I'll buy my own socks. That's fucking ridiculous.
jokes.setup: Maureen lists tasks she won't do anymore
jokes.punchline: Reveals Larry has been having his assistant buy his socks
jokes.explanation: Good character detail about Larry's helplessness with basic tasks.

jokes.text: I thought, 'this bulvan will walk in there and in two minutes she'll be out in a huff.' That's what I thought.
jokes.setup: Larry realizing Jerry set him up to fail
jokes.punchline: Jerry admitting he knew Larry would mishandle it
jokes.explanation: Good revelation of Jerry's manipulation, with 'bulvan' being a nice character-specific insult.

jokes.text: You got something you want to say to me, cuntface?
jokes.setup: Road rage incident after near-collision
jokes.punchline: Immediate escalation to extreme profanity
jokes.explanation: Shock value humor. Gets reaction but relies on extreme language rather than clever writing.

jokes.text: Honking's very dangerous. Yeah. I try never to honk. Only in dire emergencies.
jokes.setup: Road rage incident just occurred
jokes.punchline: Larry's overly cautious philosophy about honking
jokes.explanation: Sets up recurring theme. Larry's extreme caution about honking is perfectly neurotic.

jokes.text: The gentle honk. Yeah. The throat clear. The most I could do is a gentle honk. Exactly. 'Hello, excuse me.' Do you have that technique?
jokes.setup: Discussion of honking philosophy
jokes.punchline: Elaborate taxonomy of polite honking techniques
jokes.explanation: Great observational comedy about social etiquette. The detailed breakdown of honking types is clever.

jokes.text: You should bring a bath towel to eat. That's what you should bring.
jokes.setup: Larry uses too many napkins
jokes.punchline: Richard's exaggerated solution
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke about Larry's napkin usage. Visual humor.

jokes.text: I know him longer so he should sit over there, because I'm actually gonna be talking to him more than you so I should have eye contact with him as opposed to turning around and straining my neck.
jokes.setup: Restaurant seating arrangement dispute
jokes.punchline: Larry applies elaborate logic to justify getting the better seat
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry self-serving logic disguised as reasonable argument.

jokes.text: Of course I want the luxury suite. You want the luxury suite too. Yes, I do. But you...
jokes.setup: Restaurant seating dispute with Richard
jokes.punchline: Both admitting they want the best seat
jokes.explanation: Honest admission of selfish motives. Good character moment showing their pettiness.

jokes.text: Hey, where'd he go? Oh my god. What the hell? I'm fucking angry at you.
jokes.setup: Richard storms out during seating argument
jokes.punchline: Richard's dramatic exit and return
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy beat. Richard's dramatic exit works but isn't exceptional writing.

jokes.text: It's like winning an election but waiting to get sworn in. So the audition is the swearing in. It's nothing.
jokes.setup: Cheryl confused about having to audition for part she already has
jokes.punchline: Larry's elaborate political metaphor
jokes.explanation: Clever analogy that's both accurate and absurd. Good use of unexpected comparison.

jokes.text: It is sexist! It's completely sexist. We would have said that to a man if... If there was man flab.
jokes.setup: Cheryl calls out the Maureen situation as sexist
jokes.punchline: Larry defends with the concept of 'man flab'
jokes.explanation: 'Man flab' is a funny phrase but the joke is somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: Yeah, what about the guy we didn't like because he was veiny? Yeah, there was the veiny guy.
jokes.setup: Defending firing Maureen by citing other firings
jokes.punchline: Remembering firing someone for being 'veiny'
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist hiring logic. 'Veiny guy' is memorably specific and shallow.

jokes.text: That first a.d. From 'evening shade' had foamy mouth. We didn't hire him. He was bubbly. I can't work with somebody who's bubbly.
jokes.setup: Continuing list of shallow hiring rejections
jokes.punchline: Larry's aversion to 'bubbly' people
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry pettiness - rejecting someone for being too positive. Character-specific neurosis.

jokes.text: You know, the protuberance and the whole... All of it.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to explain what he's okay with about Maureen's appearance
jokes.punchline: Using 'protuberance' to describe her stomach
jokes.explanation: Brilliant word choice - 'protuberance' is both clinical and insulting. Peak Larry awkwardness.

jokes.text: Perfect world? Maybe an inch further down, cover up the belly button. That's all. Perfect world, perfect world!
jokes.setup: Larry supposedly being okay with Maureen's clothing
jokes.punchline: Still making demands while claiming acceptance
jokes.explanation: Larry can't help himself from making one more demand. The 'perfect world' repetition shows his inability to let it go.

jokes.text: You are the spitting image of my deceased husband tom. Oh my god. Oh, I'm having a hot flash.
jokes.setup: Larry meets Maureen's mother
jokes.punchline: She thinks he looks exactly like her dead husband and has physical reaction
jokes.explanation: Good absurd setup for the Tom storyline - woman having hot flash over Larry's resemblance to dead husband.

jokes.text: Oh, it happened so fast. You know, he was murdered, and... Murdered?! Yes! It was 1962. It was our wedding day.
jokes.setup: Mother explaining what happened to deceased husband Tom
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation of murder on wedding day
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy escalation. The casual delivery of horrific information creates perfect discomfort.

jokes.text: What a schmohawk. You bastard! Go ahead, pull over. Just keep going. Please, don't get in a fight.
jokes.setup: Road rage incident during honeymoon fantasy
jokes.punchline: Mixing Yiddish insults with 1962 road rage
jokes.explanation: Good callback to road rage theme. 'Schmohawk' is a nice Yiddish touch.

jokes.text: and all he did was honk! He honked! That's unbelievable. You can't honk anymore! You cannot honk.
jokes.setup: Back to present, discussing Tom's murder
jokes.punchline: Reveals Tom was killed over honking, validates Larry's horn phobia
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that retroactively justifies Larry's seemingly irrational horn fear. Perfect structure connecting setup to payoff.

jokes.text: I'll tell you what else you can't do... You can't shush. No honking, no shushing. That sound... Shh-shh, you know. It is offensive. It's startling.
jokes.setup: Building list of dangerous social behaviors
jokes.punchline: Adds shushing to the list of prohibited sounds
jokes.explanation: Good extension of the honking theme - Larry creating comprehensive list of socially dangerous sounds.

jokes.text: All bald people do not look alike.
jokes.setup: Mother insisting Larry looks exactly like dead husband Tom
jokes.punchline: Maureen's matter-of-fact reality check
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan reality check. Simple but effective punchline to the mistaken identity bit.

jokes.text: Larry, it's the invisible jew. Richard lewis, remember me?
jokes.setup: Richard calling after being ignored at lunch
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecating 'invisible jew' description
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor. Richard's neurotic self-description fits his persona perfectly.

jokes.text: Mom! You've got to see this. Come here, come here! Oh oh! Oh my god. Look at jesus! I know!
jokes.setup: They discover the 'weeping' Jesus painting
jokes.punchline: Their excitement over what they think is a miracle
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony - audience knows the truth while characters believe it's miraculous.

jokes.text: I think every erection is a miracle.
jokes.setup: Asked if he's witnessed miracles
jokes.punchline: Unexpected sexual observation in religious context
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry line - inappropriate, philosophical, and delivered deadpan. Maximum impact with minimal words.

jokes.text: I think every erection is a miracle.
jokes.setup: Maureen asking if Larry has witnessed miracles
jokes.punchline: Larry's unexpected perspective on miracles
jokes.explanation: Brilliant unexpected response that's both philosophical and crude. Perfect economy and timing.

jokes.text: You got it! Don't even think about it. That's nothing. Larry 'how can I help' david.
jokes.setup: Maureen asking Larry to cosign for RV
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately agreeing, Jerry's sarcastic nickname
jokes.explanation: Good ironic character moment - Larry being uncharacteristically helpful, Jerry's mocking nickname lands well.

jokes.text: You splashed him, didn't you? I splashed him.
jokes.setup: Jerry figuring out Larry caused the Jesus 'miracle'
jokes.punchline: Larry's matter-of-fact admission
jokes.explanation: Great setup for the big reveal. Larry's casual admission of desecration is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Only two. I put two in the bag. That's plenty. I had a handicapped kid in here yesterday... Know how many napkins he got? Two!
jokes.setup: Larry taking extra napkins
jokes.punchline: Enzo's inappropriate comparison to handicapped customer
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that works through inappropriate logic. Sets up Larry's arrest.

jokes.text: I just can't tell. Don't know which one it is? I'm confused. I just see bald.
jokes.setup: Police lineup with Larry and another bald man
jokes.punchline: Witness unable to distinguish between bald men
jokes.explanation: Visual callback to earlier joke about bald people looking alike. Solid payoff to setup.

jokes.text: I just can't tell. Don't know which one it is? I'm confused. I just see bald. I... I don't know.
jokes.setup: Larry in police lineup for napkin theft
jokes.punchline: Witness can't distinguish between bald men
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the Tom resemblance theme - all bald men looking the same saves Larry.

jokes.text: Tom! Ohhh!
jokes.setup: Road rage incident, Larry getting hit with baseball bat
jokes.punchline: Mother thinking Larry is her dead husband Tom being attacked
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that brings together multiple story threads. Physical comedy with emotional resonance.

jokes.text: Maybe tomorrow we'll dress up like kaufman and hart... Put on coats and ties, tweed jackets. Yeah, that's a good idea. Smoke some cigarettes. Maybe we'll have a good writing day. Yeah, then maybe wrestle naked like oliver reed and alan bates.
jokes.setup: Discussing dressing professionally for writing
jokes.punchline: Larry escalates to naked wrestling reference
jokes.explanation: Good absurd escalation from professional dress to naked wrestling - classic Larry non-sequitur.

jokes.text: Larry! You sprayed on jesus! What can I do? I'm taking this medication and it's getting all over the place! I can't control it!
jokes.setup: The 'miracle' tear on Jesus painting revealed
jokes.punchline: Larry's medication causing the 'miracle'
jokes.explanation: Masterful callback that recontextualizes the entire miracle subplot. Perfect Curb structure connecting opening to climax.

jokes.text: Larry, you sprayed on jesus! What can I do? I'm taking this medication and it's getting all over the place! I can't control it!
jokes.setup: Truth about the 'miracle' is revealed
jokes.punchline: Larry's desperate excuse about uncontrollable medication effects
jokes.explanation: Good climactic revelation with Larry's typically inadequate excuse for the disaster he's caused.

jokes.text: Oh, christ. Let's go... Go get her. Oh no! Please... Larry. Oh, larry! Hang on, larry. Hang on! Larry. Hold on. Ahhhh.
jokes.setup: They chase after the mother who ran away
jokes.punchline: Larry falls into a hole or pit while trying to help
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy ending - Larry literally falls into trouble trying to fix the situation he caused.

tmdb_overview: When Larry and Jerry Seinfeld get together to write the reunion show, they consider including Larry's latest experience, which involves a crying Jesus and a bare-midriff assistant.
